raw_consumer_statement:
Overview : I am reporting Citibank , N.A . for a violation of Regulation Z ( 12 CFR 1026.13 ) regarding the failure to properly credit merchant-issued refunds. Despite providing merchant-verified bank logs and authorization codes, Citibank has reached a technical impasse and closed my dispute. 

Chronology : XXXX XXXX XXXX  : Two charges were made at XXXX XXXX totaling {$6200.00} USD. 

XX/XX/2026 : XXXX acknowledged a billing error and processed two credits totaling XXXX XXXX. 

The Error : Due to the XXXX XXXX strengthening, the refund value is approximately {$6500.00} USD. 

Citi 's Failure : Because the refund amount ( ~ {$6500.00} ) is higher than the original charges ( ~ {$6200.00} ), Citibanks automated systems failed to " match '' the transactions. 

Evidence Provided : I provided Citi with the merchants bank movement logs and specific Authorization Codes : XXXX and XXXX. 

Current Status : I have an open Internal Ethics Case ( # XXXX ). I visited a retail branch on XX/XX/XXXX, where management refused to assist with this credit card compliance failure. Citibank is demanding payment of the disputed {$6200.00} by XX/XX/2026, while refusing to issue the mandatory provisional credit or conduct a manual research adjustment of the provided Authorization Codes.

Requested Resolution : Immediate manual location of the funds via the XXXX network using codes XXXX and XXXX. 

Reinstatement of provisional credit to prevent interest and late fees.
